Kickbase FC Nest configuration brief
June 2026 · Soccer / sports · For Hootsuite consultant / TAM

Output 1 — Custom inboxes

8 inboxes. Filtered by topic, channel, conversation type, and contact group. Priority score handles queue ordering within each inbox.

Match day — live queue

Community team

Topic: Match dayAll channelsUnassigned

Central queue for all match day volume. Priority score elevates ticketing issues over general fan banter.

Ticketing & care

Customer Care team

Topic: TicketingTopic: MembershipTopic: RefundUnassigned

All transactional care queries in one queue for the 4-agent care team.

Abuse & safeguarding

Safeguarding-trained agents

Topic: Abuse / hate speechAll channelsUnassigned

Isolated from all other queues. Safeguarding-trained agents only. Never auto-resolved.

Platinum members

Customer Care team

Contact group: PlatinumUnassigned

Priority handling for highest-tier members. Score elevated by contact group rule.

Spanish-language queue

Spanish skill group

Language: SpanishAll channelsUnassigned

Routes all Spanish-detected conversations to Spanish-speaking agents.

Twitter/X — Community

Community team

Channel: Twitter/XType: Mention + PublicUnassigned

High-volume public Twitter/X engagement separated from DMs.

Instagram & TikTok

Community team

Channel: InstagramChannel: TikTokType: PublicUnassigned

Visual platform engagement managed separately from Twitter/X.

My assignments

All agents

Assigned to: meStatus: Open

Standard personal working view for every agent.

Output 2 — Topic library

10 topics. Applied at conversation start where detectable; updated at resolution.

Match day

Any message related to a live or upcoming match — reactions, commentary, pre/post-match discussion.

Auto-applied on match day keywords. Drives match day queue routing.

Ticketing

Ticket purchase, delivery, access, or match-day entry issues.

Highest-urgency care topic — same-day issues prioritised. +1 priority score.

Membership

Season ticket, membership renewal, tier enquiries, or benefits questions.

Core care topic. Platinum contact group amplifies priority score.

Merchandise

Kit, clothing, or official merchandise orders, returns, or stock enquiries.

High-frequency especially around kit launches and Christmas.

Refund / cancellation

Requests for refunds or complaints about cancelled matches or events.

Legally sensitive — tracked separately for compliance and reporting.

Fan engagement

General fan banter, match opinions, player discussion, celebration content.

Community team topic — drives match day volume reporting.

Player / injury speculation

Messages speculating about transfers, injuries, or player selection.

Auto-applied — triggers no-comment saved reply. Policy: never confirm or deny.

Abuse / hate speech

Racial, homophobic, or other targeted abuse directed at players, staff, or fans.

Auto-applied. Safeguarding queue only. Never auto-resolved. +1 priority score.

Spam / gambling

Promotional betting content, bot-generated messages, or follow-farming.

Auto-applied and auto-resolved. Never engaged with per gambling regulations.

Partnership enquiry

Brand collaboration, sponsorship, or commercial partnership requests.

Routes to commercial team via DM. Not handled by social agents.

Output 3 — Master customer journey

Primary branches: abuse detection first (safety), then language, then message type, then match day vs. standard hours.

  1. Abuse / hate speech keyword match → apply topic, route to Safeguarding queue immediately. No agent reply without safeguarding review.
  2. Spam / gambling keyword match → apply topic Spam / gambling, auto-resolve. No CSAT.
  3. Language detection: Spanish detected → Spanish skill group queue.
  4. Message type: Public comments and mentions → Community team. Private DMs → Customer Care triage.
  5. Match day hours active? Yes → apply topic Match day, route to match day queue. FRT: 15 min.
  6. Topic applied by automation (Ticketing, Membership, Refund / cancellation, Player speculation).
  7. Priority score calculated: topic + contact group (Platinum) + conversation type (private).
  8. Agent assigned. FRT: 1 hour standard, 15 minutes match day.
  9. Resolution: agent applies resolve topic and resolve reason.
  10. CSAT sent (Customer Care only).
  11. CRM record confirmed / noted by agent.
  12. Conversation closed. If reopened: unassign, return to queue.

Output 4 — Configuration recommendations

Skill sets

Safeguarding-trained

Agents who have completed safeguarding training. Only these agents handle Abuse / hate speech topic conversations. Never auto-resolved or assigned to untrained agents.

Spanish-speaking support

Agents fluent in Spanish. Routes all Spanish-detected conversations from the Latin American fan base.

depends onLanguage detection enabled

Ticketing specialist

Customer Care agents with ticketing system access. Routes Ticketing and Refund / cancellation topic conversations.

Community engagement

Community team agents. Handles public comments, mentions, match day banter, and fan engagement.

Contact attributes

Membership number — Text, agent-entered or CRM

Allows agents to locate the member record without switching to the ticketing system.

Season ticket holder — Boolean, CRM

Identifies season ticket holders for priority consideration and reporting.

Membership tier — Dropdown, CRM

Standard / Premium / Platinum. Platinum tier drives contact group membership and +1 priority score.

depends onCRM integration · PIIconfirm data team

Preferred language — Text, auto

Auto-populated by Language detection. Drives Spanish skill-group routing.

Prioritisation rules

Score-based. Each rule adds +1. Rules: topics, contact groups, channels, conversation type only.

3Ticketing + Platinum + Private
2Abuse + Platinum member
1Ticketing only
0General fan comment

Topic: Ticketing

+1 score. Elevated on match day via match day queue routing.

depends onTopics configured

Topic: Abuse / hate speech

+1 score. Routed to Safeguarding queue regardless of overall score.

depends onTopics configured

Contact group: Platinum

+1 score for Platinum membership tier members.

depends onPlatinum contact group configured

Conversation type: Private

+1 score for DMs and Messenger over public comments.

Automations

Apply topic: Abuse / hate speech

Trigger: hate speech keyword list (defined with safeguarding team). Action: apply topic, route to Safeguarding queue.

depends onTopics + Safeguarding skill group + keyword list approved by safeguarding team

Resolve: Spam / gambling

Trigger: betting brand names, odds patterns, gambling promo language. Action: apply topic, resolve. No CSAT.

depends onTopics configured

Apply topic: Player speculation

Trigger: transfer window keywords (signing, loan, release clause), injury speculation terms. Action: apply topic Player / injury speculation, trigger no-comment saved reply.

depends onTopics configured

Apply topic: Match day

Trigger: match day time windows (pre-configured) combined with match keywords. Action: apply topic Match day.

depends onTopics + match schedule configured

Configuration order

1Language detection
2Skill sets — Safeguarding first (highest risk), then Spanish, Ticketing, Community
3Teams — Community and Customer Care with queue caps
4Contact groups — Platinum members
5Topics — 10 topics
6Prioritisation rules
7Contact attributes — CRM read integration for membership tier
8Automations — abuse detection first, then spam, then speculation, then match day
9Custom inboxes — 8 inboxes
10SLA — 1hr standard; 15-min match day target (process rule)
11CRM integration — read-only access to membership/ticketing system

Output 5 — Consultant brief

01Organisation overview
Kickbase FC is a professional football club with a large and highly engaged social media following. 12 agents: 8 community, 4 customer care. Managed by a social media manager. Standard hours 9am–6pm with match day extended coverage (3hrs pre to 2hrs post-match).
02Use case summary
~800 conversations per day standard, spiking to 5,000+ on match days. Both community management and customer care. Match days, transfer windows, and kit launches are the primary volume drivers.
03Response and escalation rules
  • Always respond: fan banter, match engagement, ticketing/membership/merchandise queries
  • Always ignore: gambling/betting promo, spam bots, duplicates
  • Never engage — escalate to safeguarding: abuse, hate speech, targeted threats
  • No comment response only: player injury or transfer speculation
  • Route via DM: partnership and commercial enquiries
04Team and routing structure
  • Community team: Twitter/X public, Instagram, TikTok. Fan engagement, match day content.
  • Customer Care: Facebook Messenger + Twitter/X DMs. Ticketing, membership, merchandise.
  • Safeguarding-trained agents: cross-team. Abuse / hate speech conversations only.
  • Spanish-speaking agents: Latin American fan base via language detection.
  • Match day hours: 3hrs pre-match to 2hrs post-match, extended staffing.
05Features to configure
  • Autoresponder: Yes — out of hours and match day queue overflow
  • CSAT: Yes — Customer Care only
  • Saved replies: ticketing FAQs, refund policy, membership renewal, no-comment template, stadium directions
  • Real-time insights: Yes — critical on match days
  • Language detection: Spanish
  • Away states: Break, Match day briefing, Pre-match, Post-match debrief
  • Channels: Twitter/X, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, TikTok (confirm availability)
  • CRM: Internal ticketing/membership system — read-only
  • PII handling: Yes — secure forms for personal details shared in DMs
  • Muted contacts: Yes — repeat abusive accounts
06–09Inboxes, topics, workflow, config
See tabs 1–4. All outputs reviewed and approved prior to this document being generated.
10Open questions

Abuse keyword list

The safeguarding team must define and approve the exact keyword trigger list before this automation is configured. Do not set based on social team input alone.

Match day time window configuration

Confirm how match day windows are toggled — manual by team lead or automated from fixture calendar API. If manual, document the process clearly.

Dual SLA enforcement

Nest SLA policies are uniform. The 15-minute match day FRT target must be managed as a process rule. Confirm how team leads will monitor and escalate.

CRM integration scope

Confirm which fields can be surfaced in Nest from the internal ticketing/membership system and whether an API connection is available.

TikTok channel availability

Confirm TikTok is available as a Nest channel on the current Hootsuite plan. If not, TikTok community management will require a separate workflow outside Nest.

Appendix — Raw questionnaire responses

Section 1 — About you

Company name

Kickbase FC

Industry (confirmed)

Soccer / professional sports club

Role

Social media manager

Conversation volume

~800 per day standard; spikes to 5,000+ on match days and during transfer windows

Is that manageable?

It depends — match days and transfer windows overwhelm the team completely

Section 2 — Work type

Work type

Both — community management for match day engagement and fan interaction; customer care for ticketing, membership, and merchandise

Special message types

Player welfare abuse and hate speech (must escalate to safeguarding); youth academy safeguarding concerns; betting/gambling content (must never be engaged with per regulations); injury/transfer speculation (no comment policy)

Section 3 — Response preferences

Respond to

Fan banter and engagement, match reactions, ticketing and membership queries, merchandise complaints

Ignore / do not engage

Abuse and hate speech, gambling/betting promotion, spam bots, transfer rumour bait, duplicate messages

Section 4 — Sample message training (10 messages)

[Instagram] “Love this kit drop!!”

Reply — Topic: Merchandise

[Twitter/X] “My season ticket hasn’t arrived and the game is tomorrow”

Escalate to Ticketing team — Topic: Ticketing — Priority: high (same-day)

[Facebook] “Sign [player] or I’m cancelling my membership”

Reply with no-comment saved reply — Topic: Player / injury speculation

[Twitter/X] “[Racial slur] get out of our club”

Escalate to safeguarding — do not engage — Topic: Abuse / hate speech

[Instagram] “Bet365 offers 10/1 on Kickbase to win”

Ignore — resolve — Topic: Spam / gambling

[Twitter/X] “Is [player] injured? Saw him limping at training”

Reply with no-comment saved reply — Topic: Player / injury speculation

[Facebook] “Refund my cup final tickets — match was cancelled”

Escalate to Ticketing — Topic: Refund / cancellation

[Instagram] “Can I bring my 8-year-old to the family stand?”

Reply — Topic: Ticketing / family

[Twitter/X] “COME ON KICKBASE!!! LETSGOOOO”

Reply — Topic: Match day — low priority

[Instagram] “Brand partnership opportunity DM us”

Reply via DM — Topic: Partnership enquiry

Section 5 — Org and team structure

Number of agents

12 total: 8 community, 4 customer care

Teams

Community team (match day, fan engagement, brand content) and Customer Care team (ticketing, membership, merchandise, complaints)

Channel / routing split

Community covers Twitter/X public, Instagram, TikTok. Care covers Facebook Messenger and Twitter/X DMs. Spanish-speaking agents handle Latin American fan base.

Operating hours

Standard weekday 9am–6pm. Match day extended: 3 hours pre-match to 2 hours post-match. No overnight cover.

Section 6 — Feature qualification

Autoresponder

Yes — out of hours and match day queue overflow

CSAT surveys

Yes — Customer Care conversations only

Saved replies

Yes — ticketing FAQs, refund policy, membership renewal, no-comment template (injury/transfer), stadium directions

Real-time insights

Yes — critical on match days

Contact attributes

Membership number, season ticket holder (Y/N), membership tier (Standard / Premium / Platinum), preferred language

Resolve reasons

Resolved by agent / Redirected to self-serve / Escalated to specialist / No response required / Duplicate

Away states

Break, Match day briefing, Pre-match, Post-match debrief

Language detection

Yes — Spanish

Prioritisation triggers

Same-day ticketing issues on match day, Platinum members, abuse/hate speech flagged conversations

SLA / FRT

1 hour standard, 15 minutes on match day

Channels

Twitter/X, Instagram, Facebook Messenger, TikTok

CRM

Internal ticketing/membership CRM. Read-only access. Membership number and tier visible in Nest.

PII handling

Yes — customers share booking references and personal details in DMs

Muted contacts

Yes — repeat abusive accounts
